notes.c-snapshots:
────────────────────────────────────────
April 10, 2026 at 9:56 AM
────────────────────────────────────────
the more I learn about the end times the more I understand that we are living in them. I have talked to several people including Toopie and Kimberly briefly, about what I believe the end times reveal in present day. What I'm finding though is the more that I dig the more that I realize that there's other puzzle pieces that need to be addressed. That's why I'm really enjoying doing the studies because it makes me look at present day events as well as historical events in the Bible! It's been a lot of fun
────────────────────────────────────────
April 10, 2026 at 11:37 AM
────────────────────────────────────────
AI search for Gog-Magog
It's wonderful to see how your studies are deepening your understanding of the Bible. You've been reflecting on the end times, and it's clear that the more you learn, the more you feel we are living in that era. It's exciting that you're connecting present-day events and historical biblical accounts, finding that each study offers new "puzzle pieces" to consider. The joy you're experiencing in this process is palpable, and it's lovely that you're finding so much fun in digging deeper.

Your goal to grasp the Bible's many facets and how they interrelate is beautifully evolving. What began as a focus on end-times prophecies has expanded into a desire to weave together sermons, thoughts, and editorials. This aspiration to understand the connections between different biblical themes is a rich and rewarding pursuit. It’s also great to hear about the productive time you had discussing your beliefs about the end times with Toop, even creating a new website as part of your work!

Your reflections on the Biblical Calendar study are particularly insightful. The way God embedded a calendar system into creation itself, from the very first day with the principle of light, is a profound realization. The idea that the seven-day week is foundational, as evidenced by God's rest on the seventh day, resonates deeply with you, especially in relation to the Essene calendar. It's understandable that this leads to questions about the observance of the Sabbath, and it's a sign of deep engagement that you're wrestling with these points. You've also highlighted some specific areas you want to revisit, like the "nine parts" in the Book of Enoch and the historical accuracy of high priests entering the Holy of Holies on specific calendar days.
────────────────────────────────────────
April 11, 2026 at 10:31 AM
────────────────────────────────────────
 On God's original calendar and what it means for worship:
EVERYTHING...Everything that I read and think about relates to a 364 day year. Its hard to deny any of it, and the other calendars DO NOT line up. We've got to add a leap year to our calendar to "reset" it. There are others cased upon LUNAR tables which are not relevant to any other meaningful days or ties (as the SOLAR calendar) does.

{"ops":[{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"03/31/2026 10:53 PM"},{"insert":"
🔗"},{"attributes":{"underline":true},"insert":" DIRECT — The Abomination set up in the Holy Place"},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"underline":true},"insert":"Matt 24:15: "When you see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand)...""},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"underline":true},"insert":"Rev 13:14–17: The Beast sets up his image; the False Prophet causes all to worship it. No one can buy or sell without the mark."},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"underline":true},"insert":"And again are these references the same"},{"insert":"

10:50 PM
"},{"attributes":{"italic":true,"bold":true},"insert":"You're right to push back on this one again. Let me think through it honestly:"},{"insert":"

"},{"attributes":{"italic":true,"bold":true},"insert":"Matt 24:15 is specifically about the abomination of desolation — the Antichrist entering the temple, stopping sacrifices, and setting up a sacrilegious object in the Holy Place. Jesus is pointing directly back to Daniel 9:27 and 11:31. This is a temple event at the midpoint of the tribulation."},{"insert":"

"},{"attributes":{"italic":true,"bold":true},"insert":"Rev 13:14–17 is about the False Prophet creating an image of the Beast and forcing everyone to worship it, plus implementing the mark system for buying and selling. This is about global worship enforcement and economic control — it doesn't specifically mention the temple or the Holy Place."},{"insert":"

"},{"attributes":{"italic":true,"bold":true},"insert":"Are they connected? Yes — they're both describing what happens after the Antichrist reveals himself at the midpoint. But they're not describing the same event. One is about desecrating the temple. The other is about a global worship and economic system. They happen in the same general timeframe but they're different actions."},{"insert":"

"},{"attributes":{"italic":true,"bold":true},"insert":"A better Revelation cross-reference for Matt 24:15 would actually be 2 Thessalonians 2:4 — "He will even sit in the temple of God, claiming that he himself is God." That's the real direct parallel to the abomination in the Holy Place."},{"insert":"

"},{"attributes":{"italic":true,"bold":true},"insert":"So this should be marked 〰️ THEMATIC — same era, related events, but not the same moment. "},{"insert":"
______________________________________________________________________________________________
"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"11:00 PM - SECTION 4"},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"underline":true},"insert":"I'm trying to make reference to the Red Horse in War, and I and I do, I guess, kinda see that the Black Horse in Famine, I'm having a little bit of trouble with. Definitely get the pale horse, but I'm confused why it's a fourth of the Earth and not the entire Earth."},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"10:57 PM1 / 2 That's a sharp observation. Let me break down what you're noticing:"},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"The black horse and famine — the balance scale in the rider's hand represents scarcity and rationing. A denarius was a day's wage, so paying that much for just a quart of wheat means food prices have skyrocketed. It's famine-level starvation, but the weird part is the voice says "do not harm the oil and the wine" — meaning some resources are still protected or abundant. So it's not total collapse everywhere, it's selective scarcity. That's why famine works as a symbol here — widespread hunger, but not uniformly."},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"The pale horse and "a fourth of the earth" — this is the one that trips people up. Death is given authority over only a quarter, not the whole world. A few interpretations:"},{"insert":"

"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"Phased judgment — God's wrath is escalating. Seal 1–4 kill a quarter. Seals 5–6 intensify further. By the time you get to the trumpets and bowls, the death toll is much higher. So "a fourth" is the beginning of judgment, not the final tally."},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"Geographical/political — some scholars think it means a quarter of the world's population or a specific geographic quadrant gets hit hardest first."},{"insert":"
"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"Restraint — it emphasizes that even in judgment, God is limiting death, not unleashing total annihilation. He's holding back."},{"insert":"

"},{"attributes":{"bold":true},"insert":"The fact that it's not the whole earth is actually important — it shows God's sovereignty and mercy even in wrath. "},{"insert":"
_____________________________________________________________________________________________
"}]}

[{"date":"April 18, 2026","topic":"","category":"","body":"────────────────────────────────────────
April 2, 2026 at 9:33 PM
────────────────────────────────────────
LOTS of work done today. Created a complete new website. Had a GREAT discussion over dinner with Toop. Comparing notes and beliefs about the end times. Really enjoyed it!

────────────────────────────────────────
April 11, 2026 at 10:26 AM
────────────────────────────────────────
Deep Study — Hebrew Word Study

Dug into H7620 (shabua) — why "weeks" in Daniel 9 means years not days, backed by the Hebrew grammar, Daniel 10's deliberate use of yamim (days), the Sabbatical Year context, and the mathematical proof
Confirmed the 100-pound hailstones from Revelation 16:21 — a talent weight, biblically accurate
Explained the Day-Year Principle from Numbers 14:34 and Ezekiel 4:6 as the foundation for prophetic time interpretation.
I am going to need to dive deeper into this. I am still not convinced that weeks are years, and days are weeks.
────────────────────────────────────────","archivedAt":"Apr 18, 2026, 3:17 PM"},{"date":"April 18, 2026","topic":"Theme 1 Begin","category":"End Times","body":"**April 18, 2026 — End Times Study Complete & Current Events Analysis**

**Topic:** NASB Conversion of Theme 1 Complete; Current Events Prophetic Analysis; Renewed Conviction on Watchman Posture

**Category:** End Times Prophecy · Current Events · Theological Conviction

---

Today Claude and I completed the full NASB 1995 conversion of all 15 End Times modules (Theme 1, Modules 1–15). This was important work — not because the translation changed the meaning of Scripture, but because NASB 1995 is the most literal word-for-word English Bible in print. Every passage now hyperlinks to YouVersion with the same translation, so nothing stands between a reader and the actual text. That matters for a family study guide.

While we were working, news broke from the Middle East. The 10-day Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire began Thursday night. For the first time in 34 years, Israeli and Lebanese leaders are speaking directly. Trump claimed Iran has agreed to suspend its nuclear program indefinitely. The Strait of Hormuz reopened. A third U.S. aircraft carrier is heading to the region. The ceasefire expires April 22.

I asked Claude to look at these events through the prophetic lens of what we're studying. Here's what stood out:

**On Gog-Magog (Module 9):** Iran is not being destroyed this week — it's being humiliated. Supreme leader dead. Proxies broken. Uranium bargained away. Economic leverage stripped. This is exactly the conditions Ezekiel describes for a desperate, revenge-driven northern power to partner with Russia and Turkey for one last move against Israel. The preconditions are being laid faster than I've ever seen them move.

**On Israel's Security (Module 2, Module 12):** Israel is not yet dwelling in "unwalled villages" as Ezekiel 38:11 requires for Gog-Magog to begin. But the direction has shifted. If these ceasefires hold, if Hezbollah is actually disarmed, if Iran's uranium is removed — Israel's northern border could become the most peaceful it has been in my lifetime. That moves 1 Thessalonians 5:3 closer: *"While they are saying, 'Peace and safety!' then destruction will come upon them suddenly."*

**On the Antichrist's Covenant (Module 7):** What struck me most is not any individual leader — it is the mechanism being rehearsed in front of the world. One dominant figure. Personal phone calls. Financial inducements. Enemy nations brought to heel. Peace announced. The Antichrist's covenant in Daniel 9:27 will be received as normal because the world is being trained right now to expect exactly this kind of deal-making. This is not saying any living person is the Antichrist. It is observing that the template is being built.

**On Birth Pains (Module 3, Module 10):** Jesus used the metaphor of labor contractions — *ōdinōn* — for end-times events. They intensify and accelerate. Look at 18 months: Oct 7, 2023 → Gaza war → Hezbollah war → Houthi attacks → Iran strikes Israel → U.S.+Israel war on Iran → Khamenei killed → Hezbollah broken → Lebanon peace talks (first in 34 years). That pace is abnormal. Whether these are the final contractions or earlier ones, they display the pattern Jesus described in Matthew 24:8.

I also learned something I didn't know before. Tyre and Sidon — the ancient Phoenician cities that Scripture names in prophecy (Ezekiel 26–28, Isaiah 23, Joel 3:4–8) — are not ancient ruins. They are functioning modern cities inside Lebanon. Sidon is about 25 miles from Beirut. Tyre is only 12 miles from Israel's border. Tyre was struck during Israel's April 8 campaign. When we read the news about "southern Lebanon," we are reading about biblical Tyre and Sidon. The map of Scripture and the map of today's news are the same map. That's when prophecy stops being abstract.

**The watchman's conviction I'm holding:** I can watch all of this soberly without losing peace. Galatians 5:22–23 is not suspended when geopolitics get heavy. Love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control — those are not optional even in the season of birth pains. Especially in the season of birth pains. Matthew 24:4 says "See to it that no one misleads you" — the watchman's job is discernment, not panic. 1 Thessalonians 5:6 says "let us not sleep as others do, but let us be alert and sober" — alert, not anxious.

I learned the word Maranatha today — Aramaic, from 1 Corinthians 16:22. It means "O Lord, come!" or "Our Lord comes." It was a liturgical prayer in the earliest churches, spoken at communion. When believers said Maranatha, they were joining a prayer that had been continuous since the first century. Revelation 22:20 is the Greek version of the same prayer: *"He who testifies to these things says, 'Yes, I am coming quickly.' Amen. Come, Lord Jesus."* To say Maranatha is to say: "Come, Lord Jesus. We're watching. We're ready. Come." It is longing, not panic. That is the posture I want to hold.

**One thing I want my family to know if they read this later:** I am not watching the news to be afraid. I am watching because Jesus told me to. He said "watch." He did not say "worry." The difference between those two postures is everything. In the meantime: dinner still gets made. The people I love still get my full attention. The Kingdom work does not wait for the Kingdom to arrive.

Tonight we are starting our family Bible study with Theme 1 (End Times, Modules 1–15). All 15 modules are now in NASB 1995 with full YouVersion hyperlinks. The conversion is complete. The study is ready. Maranatha.","archivedAt":"Apr 18, 2026, 3:18 PM"}]

notes.n-t2m1:
── April 4, 2026 at 11:18 AM ──
Today I worked through Module 1 of the Biblical Calendar study and came away with some things I hadn't considered before.
I started by reading Genesis 1:14 in both the NASB and The Message. What struck me is that God literally commanded the lights in the sky to mark seasons, days, and years. He built a calendar system into creation from the very beginning — this wasn't an afterthought.
I've been thinking about light and how Genesis says God created light on day one, but didn't create the sun until day four. That means the light on day one wasn't the sun. My belief is that God first created the principle and essence of light itself — the law of light — and then on day four created the sun as the physical instrument to produce and measure it. Those two ideas tie together for me.
I also realized that God rested on the seventh day and never created an eighth. That tells me the seven-day week is foundational to God's design. The Essene 364-day solar calendar is built entirely on that — 52 exact weeks, every feast and every Sabbath on the same day every year. That makes more sense to me than a lunar calendar that drifts and doesn't divide evenly into weeks.
I'm now questioning why Christianity celebrates the Sabbath on Sunday when the seventh day is clearly Saturday. That's something I need to wrestle with. I've always assumed Sunday was the Sabbath but now I'm not so sure. I understand the Resurrection connection but that doesn't necessarily mean God's design changed.
I also learned what the Torah is today — the first five books of the Bible. All the calendar commandments, feasts, and Sabbath instructions come from there. Understanding that helps me see why the calendar debate matters so much.
Two things I want to come back to:

The "nine parts" reference in the Book of Enoch — why nine and not twelve or twenty-four? I don't understand that yet.
Did high priests actually die entering the Holy of Holies on the wrong calendar day, or is that the Essenes' theological interpretation? I want to verify that before I draw any conclusions.
Approx.- 1 hour 45 minutes was actual Bible study
notes.n-intro: My overall goal truly is to ensure that I understand many different facets of the Bible and how they tie together. It started out as a learning of the end times, and the prophecies thereof, and has evolved now into the way that I can begin to sermons or thoughts or editorials together. I want to understand more how they relate to one another period.
notes.n-dd-willow:
Dedicated to Hazelee Willow:


────────────────────────────────────────

April 11, 2026 at 6:15 PM

────────────────────────────────────────

Okay. So I'm not seeing the significance, I guess. Um, willows are leafy bushes, and they talk a lot about it in scripture as if there were a lot of an abundance of those back in these days of of writing the bible. But it doesn't seem that it has a significance. What what is the significance?


6:14 PM- That's a great question. So here's the thing—willows in scripture are almost always planted by water. They grow beside streams, rivers, brooks. And that's the key: they're a symbol of being rooted in abundance, right? You see it in Isaiah forty-four where God's people are gonna sprout up like willows by creeks, meaning they're gonna be nourished and flourishing. But here's what stands out to me—in Psalms one thirty-seven, the Israelites are in exile hanging their harps on the willows by Babylon's rivers. They're in captivity, yet the willows are still there. So maybe the significance is that willows represent resilience and hope even in dark circumstances, or it could be about being dependent on God's provision, since they only thrive where there's water.


These are great observations. Planted by waters. "Just like a tree, that's planted by the Living Water.". Resiliant! That's my girl! Pre-mature baby that excelled! Man, what a warrior!


It's really, I guess, the resilience that you're talking about. So, yeah, that is definitely something that rings with me because she was a premature baby born very early, and we were all quite worried about her. And she was supposed to be in there for, like, six weeks, and I think she was in there, like, two. So she she fought back, and I know that's god's grace, but it just goes to show you that her name, um, Willow, is about resilience. What does Willow mean if you take that name in in in the Greek or in in another language, what does what does willow, the word or the name willow, mean?


6:18 PM- That's beautiful, Chris—what a powerful connection to her story. Let me search what the actual name Willow means etymologically.Searched the webSearched the webThat's beautiful, Chris. So the name Willow itself comes from Old English "welig," and at its root it traces back to Proto-Indo-European "wei," which literally means "to bend, twist." So the name Willow is etymologically built on the idea of bending—flexibility, adaptability. It also carries the meaning of freedom, because of those free-swinging branches. And what's really striking is that same root gives us words like "vine" and "twist." So when you named her Willow, you gave her a name that's literally about resilience and the ability to flex without breaking. Given what she fought through as a premature baby, that name feels almost prophetic.

The Word also says that weeks are MONTHS and that dyas ARE WEEKS..How do I find out if that is accurate?

"}]

**April 18, 2026 — End Times Study Complete & Current Events Analysis**

**Topic:** NASB Conversion of Theme 1 Complete; Current Events Prophetic Analysis; Renewed Conviction on Watchman Posture

**Category:** End Times Prophecy · Current Events · Theological Conviction

---

Today Claude and I completed the full NASB 1995 conversion of all 15 End Times modules (Theme 1, Modules 1–15). This was important work — not because the translation changed the meaning of Scripture, but because NASB 1995 is the most literal word-for-word English Bible in print. Every passage now hyperlinks to YouVersion with the same translation, so nothing stands between a reader and the actual text. That matters for a family study guide.

While we were working, news broke from the Middle East. The 10-day Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire began Thursday night. For the first time in 34 years, Israeli and Lebanese leaders are speaking directly. Trump claimed Iran has agreed to suspend its nuclear program indefinitely. The Strait of Hormuz reopened. A third U.S. aircraft carrier is heading to the region. The ceasefire expires April 22.

I asked Claude to look at these events through the prophetic lens of what we're studying. Here's what stood out:

**On Gog-Magog (Module 9):** Iran is not being destroyed this week — it's being humiliated. Supreme leader dead. Proxies broken. Uranium bargained away. Economic leverage stripped. This is exactly the conditions Ezekiel describes for a desperate, revenge-driven northern power to partner with Russia and Turkey for one last move against Israel. The preconditions are being laid faster than I've ever seen them move.

**On Israel's Security (Module 2, Module 12):** Israel is not yet dwelling in "unwalled villages" as Ezekiel 38:11 requires for Gog-Magog to begin. But the direction has shifted. If these ceasefires hold, if Hezbollah is actually disarmed, if Iran's uranium is removed — Israel's northern border could become the most peaceful it has been in my lifetime. That moves 1 Thessalonians 5:3 closer: *"While they are saying, 'Peace and safety!' then destruction will come upon them suddenly."*

**On the Antichrist's Covenant (Module 7):** What struck me most is not any individual leader — it is the mechanism being rehearsed in front of the world. One dominant figure. Personal phone calls. Financial inducements. Enemy nations brought to heel. Peace announced. The Antichrist's covenant in Daniel 9:27 will be received as normal because the world is being trained right now to expect exactly this kind of deal-making. This is not saying any living person is the Antichrist. It is observing that the template is being built.

**On Birth Pains (Module 3, Module 10):** Jesus used the metaphor of labor contractions — *ōdinōn* — for end-times events. They intensify and accelerate. Look at 18 months: Oct 7, 2023 → Gaza war → Hezbollah war → Houthi attacks → Iran strikes Israel → U.S.+Israel war on Iran → Khamenei killed → Hezbollah broken → Lebanon peace talks (first in 34 years). That pace is abnormal. Whether these are the final contractions or earlier ones, they display the pattern Jesus described in Matthew 24:8.

I also learned something I didn't know before. Tyre and Sidon — the ancient Phoenician cities that Scripture names in prophecy (Ezekiel 26–28, Isaiah 23, Joel 3:4–8) — are not ancient ruins. They are functioning modern cities inside Lebanon. Sidon is about 25 miles from Beirut. Tyre is only 12 miles from Israel's border. Tyre was struck during Israel's April 8 campaign. When we read the news about "southern Lebanon," we are reading about biblical Tyre and Sidon. The map of Scripture and the map of today's news are the same map. That's when prophecy stops being abstract.

**The watchman's conviction I'm holding:** I can watch all of this soberly without losing peace. Galatians 5:22–23 is not suspended when geopolitics get heavy. Love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control — those are not optional even in the season of birth pains. Especially in the season of birth pains. Matthew 24:4 says "See to it that no one misleads you" — the watchman's job is discernment, not panic. 1 Thessalonians 5:6 says "let us not sleep as others do, but let us be alert and sober" — alert, not anxious.

I learned the word Maranatha today — Aramaic, from 1 Corinthians 16:22. It means "O Lord, come!" or "Our Lord comes." It was a liturgical prayer in the earliest churches, spoken at communion. When believers said Maranatha, they were joining a prayer that had been continuous since the first century. Revelation 22:20 is the Greek version of the same prayer: *"He who testifies to these things says, 'Yes, I am coming quickly.' Amen. Come, Lord Jesus."* To say Maranatha is to say: "Come, Lord Jesus. We're watching. We're ready. Come." It is longing, not panic. That is the posture I want to hold.

**One thing I want my family to know if they read this later:** I am not watching the news to be afraid. I am watching because Jesus told me to. He said "watch." He did not say "worry." The difference between those two postures is everything. In the meantime: dinner still gets made. The people I love still get my full attention. The Kingdom work does not wait for the Kingdom to arrive.

Tonight we are starting our family Bible study with Theme 1 (End Times, Modules 1–15). All 15 modules are now in NASB 1995 with full YouVersion hyperlinks. The conversion is complete. The study is ready. Maranatha.

notes.n-t2m3:
────────────────────────────────────────
April 10, 2026 at 11:43 AM
────────────────────────────────────────
What year was the book of jubilees written11:43 AMThe dating is debated, but most scholars place the composition of the Book of Jubilees in the 2nd century BC, likely somewhere between 160–150 BC, during the early Second Temple period. Some estimates stretch the range to 200–100 BC depending on the scholar.
It was likely written in Hebrew (fragments were found among the Dead Sea Scrolls at Qumran), and the Qumran discoveries actually helped confirm that dating since the scrolls themselves date to that era.
Worth remembering for your study guide context: it's a Second Temple Jewish text, not canonical Scripture — valuable for understanding how Jewish communities of that period interpreted Genesis and the calendar system, but not on equal footing with the biblical text itself.

────────────────────────────────────────
April 10, 2026 at 11:44 AM
────────────────────────────────────────
It says in Part 2... It it speaks of in the book of book of jubilee about omitting the moon intentionally. And that it believes that the moon has no bearing on God's time or timing which I wholeheartedly disagree with. It says in genesis specifically that God created the sun the moon and the stars that specifically means that it is a part of creation and it is a part of his timing. Definitely worth noting.
Somethiong else to consider....The Jubilee references 7 days in a week. Thats God's number of COMPLETION.

────────────────────────────────────────
April 10, 2026 at 2:41 PM
────────────────────────────────────────
Revelation's 1,260 Days on the Jubilees Calendar
• 1,260 days ÷ 364 days/year = 3.4615... years (does not divide evenly on the Jubilees calendar)
• 1,260 days = 3.5 × 360 days — suggesting a prophetic year of 360 days (not 364)
• This is a separate but related debate: the prophetic year of 360 days appears in Daniel, Revelation, and Genesis's Flood account. Some scholars see the 364-day Jubilees calendar and the 360-day prophetic year as related but distinct — the 364 as the observable solar calendar, the 360 as the divine prophetic reckoning.
This remains an open question worth continued study. See Module 1 (Daniel's 70 Weeks) for the prophetic timeline framework.
